name: inclusion-list-basic
description: A pure inclusion-list group admits exactly its listed members (spec §9.1).
subject: subject

name: inclusion-overrides-criteria
description: The identity-membership shortcut: a listed profile is eligible regardless of criteria it fails; an unlisted profile must pass the criteria (spec §9.1).
subject: subject

name: exclusion-ban-precedence
description: Exclusion overrides everything: passing criteria and even being in the inclusion list (spec §9.2).
subject: subject

name: exclusion-only-group
description: A group with only an exclusion list admits everyone who is not excluded (spec §9.3).
subject: subject

name: exclusion-with-criteria
description: Exclusion composes with criteria: non-excluded profiles still have to pass the criteria (spec §9.2).
subject: subject

name: empty-group-divergence
description: Degenerate group with no criteria and no lists: spec says nobody is eligible; the generated SQL view contains every identity.
subject: subject

sql.note: SQL degenerates to select * from identities; spec admits nobody through an empty rule (eligibility-spec.md §9.4, §12 D-7)

name: invisible-group-divergence
description: visible=0 disqualifies the group for everyone per spec; the member-set SQL builder does not check visibility and produces the would-be member set.
subject: subject

sql.note: UserGroupsDb.getById enforces only the privacy gate, so member-set SQL is built for invisible groups (eligibility-spec.md §10, §12 D-9)
